description: Agilent E4436B ESG-DP digital signal generator (250 kHz to 3 GHz), part of the ESG-D/ESG-DP family. SCPI over GPIB. IMPORTANT: The only SCPI command reference available in the local manual library is the Options UN8/202 programming guide (E4436B UN8.pdf), which documents the digital/IQ modulation [:SOURce]:RADio subsystem plus a few common commands. The base-instrument analog subsystems (FREQuency, POWer, OUTPut, AM, FM, PM, SWEep/LIST, ROSCillator, UNIT, DISPlay, SYSTem, STATus) live in the separate ESG-D/DP SCPI Programming Guide, which is NOT present in the manual library; those commands are therefore NOT included here to avoid inventing undocumented syntax. The E4436B User.pdf is a front-panel/softkey operation reference and contains no SCPI command tables. Throughout the RADio subsystem the programming guide uses the placeholder <format> to mean any one of the supported digital formats: CUSTom, GSM, EDGE, PDC, PHS, DECT, TETRa, NADC.

identity.description: Standard IEEE 488.2 identification query. Returns manufacturer, model, serial number and firmware revision.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Resets the signal generator to a factory-defined instrument state. Documented throughout the UN8/202 programming guide as the reset preset.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears the status byte, the data questionable event register, and the error queue.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Triggers the signal generator when the trigger source (e.g. a RADio format TRIGger[:SOURce]) is set to BUS.

commands.name: radio_format_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the selected digital modulation format (CUSTom, GSM, EDGE, PDC, PHS, DECT, TETRa or NADC). Turning a format on activates the corresponding IQ/digital modulation.

commands.name: radio_format_secondary_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the secondary (alternate) framing/configuration for the selected format.

commands.name: radio_modulation_type
commands.description: Selects the digital modulation type applied to the selected format. The full set of choices documented for CUSTom is shown in the range.

commands.name: radio_modulation_fsk_deviation
commands.description: Sets the frequency deviation used when the modulation type is an FSK format.

commands.name: radio_modulation_msk_phase
commands.description: Sets the phase deviation used when the modulation type is MSK.

commands.name: radio_modulation_user_iq
commands.description: Selects a user-defined I/Q modulation map from a named file.

commands.name: radio_modulation_user_fsk
commands.description: Selects a user-defined FSK modulation map from a named file.

commands.name: radio_filter
commands.description: Selects the baseband filter applied to the selected format, or a user filter from a named file.

commands.name: radio_filter_alpha
commands.description: Sets the filter alpha (roll-off factor) for Nyquist and root-Nyquist filters.

commands.name: radio_filter_bbt
commands.description: Sets the bandwidth-bit-time (BbT) product for Gaussian filters.

commands.name: radio_symbol_rate
commands.description: Sets the transmission symbol rate for the selected format.

commands.name: radio_bit_rate
commands.description: Sets the transmission bit rate for the selected format.

commands.name: radio_data
commands.description: Selects the data pattern used to modulate the carrier: a PRBS (PNn), a fixed pattern (FIX4/Pn), or external data (EXT).

commands.name: radio_data_differential_encode
commands.description: Enables or disables differential encoding of the modulating data.

commands.name: radio_repeat
commands.description: Selects single or continuous transmission of the data pattern.

commands.name: radio_baseband_clock
commands.description: Selects the baseband data clock source.

commands.name: radio_burst_shape_type
commands.description: Selects the burst rise/fall shape, either the built-in SINE shape or a user shape from a named file.

commands.name: radio_burst_shape_rise_time
commands.description: Sets the burst rise time.

commands.name: radio_burst_shape_rise_delay
commands.description: Sets the burst rise delay.

commands.name: radio_burst_shape_fall_time
commands.description: Sets the burst fall time.

commands.name: radio_burst_shape_fall_delay
commands.description: Sets the burst fall delay.

commands.name: radio_trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the trigger source that starts transmission for the selected format: front-panel key, external trigger, or GPIB bus (*TRG).

commands.name: radio_secondary_trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the trigger source for the secondary configuration of the selected format.

commands.name: radio_trigger_external_type
commands.description: Selects the behaviour of the external trigger: continuous gating, single shot, or reset.

commands.name: radio_trigger_external_delay
commands.description: Sets the external trigger delay value.

commands.name: radio_trigger_external_delay_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the external trigger delay.

commands.name: trigger_sequence_slope
commands.description: Selects the active edge (rising or falling) of the external trigger input.

commands.name: radio_channel_optimization
commands.description: Optimizes the signal for either error vector magnitude (EVM) or adjacent channel power (ACP) performance.

commands.name: radio_polarity
commands.description: Sets the polarity of the data/clock for the selected format.

commands.name: radio_format_band
commands.description: Selects the frequency channel band for cellular formats (EDGE/GSM band selection shown; DECT/PHS/TETRa use STANdard).

commands.name: radio_format_default
commands.description: Restores the selected format to its default framing/parameter values.
